refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a persistent, auditable baseline, and provide managed updates with traceability to source worksite inputs and calibration events.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked specificity on persistence, auditability, and traceability—critical for safety-critical systems. Refinement adds explicit mention of safety margins (high safety criticality), baseline persistence, and traceability linkage to worksite inputs and calibration. This aligns with the acceptance criterion requiring 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintains a history of baselines/calibration updates.'
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs; (2) baseline records include timestamp, source inputs, and derivation method; (3) calibration updates are logged with delta and justification; (4) traceability chain from worksite input → derived target → baseline record is auditable; (5) safety margins are explicitly included in derived targets.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (site layout, task type, soil/material properties, operator skill level, safety constraints) as input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-aware algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance targets and baseline metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, safety margin justification) in persistent storage.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Manage baseline versions and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, when, why, delta).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability queries linking any performance target back to originating worksite input and calibration event.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine—a dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission context via standardized API, executes parameterized algorithms (cycle-time model, energy model, availability model) with embedded safety margin logic, and writes baseline records to a versioned, auditable database with full traceability metadata.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation—embed performance derivation logic in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules independently, each computing targets locally and syncing to a shared baseline store; rely on eventual consistency and manual reconciliation.

DP-C: Hybrid Derivation with Safety Services—delegate safety-margin computation and validation to Safety Services module; delegate nominal performance computation to Vehicle; coordinate via SafetyAlert and Tasking interfaces; persist in shared baseline store.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with mission-context ingestion API, parameterized multi-model computation (cycle-time, energy, availability, safety-margin), and versioned, auditable baseline database with full traceability metadata.

potential_sources: Coupling to worksite mission context ingestion (requires well-defined input schema and API contract).
Coupling to baseline database schema and versioning strategy (must be stable and extensible).
Coupling to safety margin computation logic (must align with Safety Services domain expertise).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to Vehicle and Teleoperation operational logic; it is primarily a data-transformation and persistence service. Coupling is primarily to input schema (worksite context) and output schema (baseline record). This is acceptable and manageable via interface contracts. Safety margin computation should be validated with Safety Services stakeholder early.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema) that is stable across different worksite types.
Assumption: Performance derivation algorithms (cycle-time, energy, availability models) are well-understood and can be parameterized by worksite context.
Risk: If worksite context schema is poorly defined or highly variable, derivation engine will require frequent updates and may produce unreliable targets.
Risk: If safety margin computation is not properly validated with Safety Services, derived targets may not meet safety criticality requirements.
Risk: Baseline database schema changes could break traceability if versioning strategy is not carefully managed.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Recommend early engagement with Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders to validate: (1) safety margin computation logic and thresholds; (2) baseline record schema and audit trail requirements; (3) worksite mission context schema and completeness. Consider prototyping the mission-context ingestion API and baseline database schema before full implementation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. CR-NGHE-005 performance derivation, baseline management, and traceability are system-level concerns that span Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Assigning to NGHE Solution reflects that this is a system-level capability, not localized to a single subsystem. The Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A) shall be realized as a dedicated software component within NGHE Solution, with well-defined interfaces to Vehicle (for operational telemetry), Teleoperation (for mission context), and Safety Services (for safety margin validation).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules provide operational telemetry (cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics) to Performance Derivation Engine for baseline computation and validation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation module provides mission context (task type, site layout, operator inputs, constraints) to Performance Derivation Engine for performance target derivation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services module provides safety margin thresholds and validates derived safety margins; Performance Derivation Engine queries SafetyAlert for safety constraints and reports derived safety margins back to Safety Services for audit.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Performance Derivation Engine may require network connectivity to retrieve worksite mission context from remote sources or to synchronize baseline records across distributed NGHE Solution instances.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary. Performance derivation, baseline management, and traceability are system-level concerns that logically belong at the top level. Minimal diffusion risk: the Performance Derivation Engine is a cohesive, self-contained service with well-defined inputs (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) and outputs (baseline records, traceability queries). No duplication or scattering across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.

Vehicle module is responsible for vehicle control, propulsion, and operational telemetry. Performance derivation is a system-level concern, not a vehicle-specific concern. Assigning to Vehicle would couple system-level performance management to vehicle subsystem, creating tight coupling and diffusion risk. Vehicle should provide telemetry inputs to Performance Derivation Engine, not own the derivation logic.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation, mission tasking, and operator interface. While Teleoperation provides mission context inputs, performance derivation is a system-level concern, not a teleoperation-specific concern. Assigning to Teleoperation would couple system-level performance management to teleoperation subsystem, creating tight coupling and diffusion risk. Teleoperation should provide Tasking inputs to Performance Derivation Engine, not own the derivation logic.

Safety Services module is responsible for safety monitoring, alerts, and safety-critical decision-making. Safety margin computation is a component of performance derivation, but the full derivation (cycle time, energy, availability, safety margins) is a system-level concern. Assigning to Safety Services would fragment performance derivation across Safety Services and another module, creating diffusion risk. Safety Services should validate and provide safety margin thresholds to Performance Derivation Engine, not own the full derivation logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance derivation, baseline management, and traceability are system-level concerns that require coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The Performance Derivation Engine should be realized as a dedicated, cohesive software component within NGHE Solution, with well-defined interfaces to subsystems. This avoids diffusion and maintains clear separation of concerns: Vehicle provides telemetry, Teleoperation provides mission context, Safety Services provides safety constraints, and NGHE Solution orchestrates derivation and baseline management.
containment_risks: Risk: If Performance Derivation Engine is not properly isolated as a dedicated component, derivation logic may diffuse into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services, creating duplication and maintenance burden.
Risk: If interfaces between Performance Derivation Engine and subsystems (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) are not well-defined, integration complexity and coupling will increase.
Risk: If baseline database schema and versioning strategy are not carefully managed, traceability may be lost or corrupted during updates.
Risk: If worksite mission context schema is not stable or well-documented, Performance Derivation Engine may require frequent updates, increasing maintenance burden.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Recommend: (1) Define detailed interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert inputs to Performance Derivation Engine; (2) Design baseline database schema with explicit versioning, audit trail, and traceability metadata; (3) Establish governance for worksite mission context schema evolution; (4) Assign ownership to a dedicated System-level Performance Management Team within NGHE Solution; (5) Plan for early integration testing with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to validate interface contracts and data flow.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses the refined FR: it provides a method to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context, record them in a persistent, auditable baseline, and manage updates with traceability. The DP is specific, implementable, and traceable to the FR. No decoupling or missing functionality identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk if Performance Derivation Engine logic is scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services instead of centralized in NGHE Solution. Mitigation: enforce strict module boundaries and interface contracts.
Diffusion risk if baseline database schema is not centralized and versioned. Mitigation: establish single source of truth for baseline records and audit trail.

Diffusion risk if safety margin computation is duplicated in Safety Services and Performance Derivation Engine. Mitigation: Safety Services provides safety margin thresholds via SafetyAlert interface; Performance Derivation Engine applies thresholds and reports results back to Safety Services for validation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for system-level performance derivation and baseline management. The Performance Derivation Engine is a cohesive, self-contained service with well-defined inputs (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) and outputs (baseline records, traceability queries). Interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are clear and manageable. No significant containment risks identified if interface contracts are well-defined and enforced.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) unit tests of derivation algorithms with synthetic worksite mission context; (2) integration tests with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to validate data flow and interface contracts; (3) audit trail inspection to confirm baseline records include timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, and safety margin justification; (4) traceability queries to confirm linkage from performance target back to originating worksite input and calibration event; (5) acceptance testing with Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders to confirm derived targets are accurate and usable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns performance derivation, baseline management, and traceability. Vehicle provides telemetry inputs. Teleoperation provides mission context inputs. Safety Services provides safety margin thresholds and validation. Interfaces are named (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and directions are specified (in/out/inout). Ownership and responsibilities are unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Recommend early engagement with Safety Manager to validate safety margin computation logic and thresholds before implementation.
Recommend early engagement with Maintenance/Quality stakeholder to define baseline record schema, audit trail requirements, and traceability metadata.
Recommend prototyping worksite mission context schema and Performance Derivation Engine API before full implementation to validate assumptions and reduce integration risk.
Recommend establishing governance for worksite mission context schema evolution to prevent schema drift and maintain stability.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 refined FR is not atomic; it comprises five sub-FRs (mission context ingestion, performance computation, baseline recording, baseline management, traceability). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to identify specific DPs and IMs. DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) is a valid solution concept, but its realization requires detailed design of: (1) mission context ingestion API and schema; (2) performance derivation algorithms (cycle-time, energy, availability models); (3) baseline database schema and versioning strategy; (4) traceability query interface; (5) safety margin computation and validation logic. These details are best addressed at the next decomposition level. Proceeding to next level will clarify implementation details, reduce integration risk, and enable detailed design and verification planning.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (site layout, task type, soil/material properties, operator skill level, safety constraints) as input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-aware algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance targets and baseline metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, safety margin justification) in persistent storage.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Manage baseline versions and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, when, why, delta).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability queries linking any performance target back to originating worksite input and calibration event.

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema) that is stable across different worksite types.
Performance derivation algorithms (cycle-time, energy, availability models) are well-understood and can be parameterized by worksite context.
Safety margin computation logic is well-defined and can be validated with Safety Services stakeholder.
Baseline database schema and versioning strategy can be designed to maintain full traceability and audit trail.
Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules can provide required inputs (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) via well-defined interfaces.
open_risks: Risk: Worksite mission context schema may be poorly defined or highly variable, requiring frequent updates to Performance Derivation Engine.
Risk: Performance derivation algorithms may not be sufficiently accurate or may require extensive tuning for different worksite types.
Risk: Safety margin computation logic may not be properly validated with Safety Services, resulting in derived targets that do not meet safety criticality requirements.
Risk: Baseline database schema changes could break traceability if versioning strategy is not carefully managed.
Risk: Integration complexity with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services may be higher than anticipated if interface contracts are not well-defined.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema definition and examples from multiple worksite types.
Performance derivation algorithm specifications (cycle-time, energy, availability models) with validation data.
Safety margin computation logic and thresholds, validated with Safety Services stakeholder.
Baseline database schema design with versioning and audit trail strategy.
Interface contract specifications for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Acceptance criteria and verification plan from Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality, and Safety Manager stakeholders.
